advantages: Preserves original feature names and units—easier for stakeholders to interpret than PCA components or latent embeddings.
Can cut training and inference cost by removing irrelevant or redundant columns before or during modeling.
Filter methods scale cheaply to very wide data and provide a quick sanity check before expensive wrappers.
Embedded L1 and wrapper RFE tie selection to predictive performance of a concrete model class.
disadvantages: Searching subsets is combinatorial; wrappers like RFE multiply training cost and can overfit the validation set if tried exhaustively.
Filter scores ignore feature interactions—two weak individual features may jointly be strong.
Correlated features make rankings unstable; L1 may pick one arbitrary member of a correlated group.
Selection on the full dataset before splitting causes leakage; wrong validation invalidates claimed gains.
